author: PC Gamer
text:
Halloween teases single-player slash 'em up campaign in grisly new trailer
Taking shape.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Exodus somehow looks even more like Mass Effect than it did before it dropped a big gameplay reveal at the Future Game Show
It's not bad, to be clear, just really familiar.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Vampire Survivors spin-offs are 'Survivatons,' its creator has decided, and another will be revealed soon
Plus there's a small new DLC add-on for Vampire Survivors on the way.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Gothic devs are 'monitoring the sentiment about lockpicking,' promise a PC patch for crashes by Monday
"We cannot stress enough how much easier this gets when you start adding skill points into it."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Wardogs is a '100% player driven' modern military shooter for up to 100 players that's 'not a battle royale' or an extraction shooter
The "all-out warfare" FPS is coming to Steam later this year.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Three days before launch, one of the most wishlisted games on Steam warns players not to expect 'a huge survival game' with 'hundreds of hours of main progression'
Instead expect a "calm, cozy, focused survival craft sandbox made by a tiny team" says developer Cyberwave.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Question time: How often do you actually talk to strangers in MMOs?
Solo or hello?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Guild Wars 1 and 2 will continue to get updates even after Guild Wars 3 is out: 'We are not replacing your favorite games'
"We are not repeating the same approach as we did all those years ago," when Guild Wars 2 launched, says ArenaNet.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Guild Wars 2 review (2012)
It promises a revolution, but can Guild Wars 2 really set the online world to rights?

author: PC Gamer
text:
PC Gamers, come rate my videogame vinyl collection and decide whether it was worth the $$$
Collecting your favorite game soundtracks can be a costly endeavour.

author: PC Gamer
text:
DLSS 5 isn't the first time the future of videogame graphics looked like total balls
Tracing the brief history of ellipsoid graphics.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Indie devs mixed on Steam's latest redesign over visibility complaints
Some claim it will hurt indie visibility, others report improvement.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Left 4 Dead meets friendslop as you and your pals try to escape 'the galaxy's most-unregulated waterpark'
Don't pee in the pool.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fourleaf Fields is an adorable little farming sim with a spill-the-tea mechanic
Your secret is not safe with me.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The vampire farm sim I've been anticipating for 3 years just released an even bigger, better demo for everyone to try before it launches next month
You can now play the same big demo for Moonlight Peaks that I got to try last month.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I'm finally getting married in Fields of Mistria when its 1.0 update launches in August
The full launch version of Fields of Mistria just announced its release date so now I've got a lot of playing left to prepare for it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Gothic Remake's lockpicking system has broken me and I can no longer experience joy
I might just give up on my life of crime.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Call of Duty's new DMZ extraction mode is a 'full-featured game within Modern Warfare 4'
Complete with quests, story missions, and a home base you build up over time.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I thought text-only Grand Theft Auto roleplay sounded too intimidating—but GTA World is showing me why players are slowing things down
Text-only GTA RP is a different beast, but with patience it's oh so rewarding

author: PC Gamer
text:
Star Citizen's latest alpha patch adds 385 asteroid defence missions, but players are reporting problems with broken hangar doors and exploding pilots
Alpha 4.8.1 gives players the chance to earn 'unique blueprints for the first time', if they can actually complete the missions.

author: PC Gamer
text:
2026's weirdest tech leak involves scuba diving, a Caribbean island, and Gearbox head honcho Randy Pitchford, and no, it's not what you expect
Water reveal.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Reinventing the PC' is the concept Nvidia wanted to get across this Computex, but I'm not sure the AI room is being read
Your agentic AI PC is coming.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bloodlines 2's final DLC introduces swordfighting, dual pistol wielding, and other stuff that should probably have been in the base game
The Flower and The Flame tells the story of glamorous Toreador Ysabella.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The UK government is considering banning children from speaking to strangers in games like Fortnite and Roblox
The UK's online safety minister is concerned about 'stranger pairing' in games.

author: PC Gamer
text:
An 8-bit Cuphead game is being programmed in assembly language for the Sega Master System
It'll be playable on modern PCs, too, and there's another "hand-animated" Cuphead game in development alongside it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
7 years after it was announced, The Wolf Among Us 2 is finally set to arrive—in 2027
A remastered version of the original Wolf Among Us is also coming later this, so you'll have something to do while you wait.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I really want to know what the cube is all about in Haex
Cubes 'n' co-op. Sure, I'll bite.

author: PC Gamer
text:
These indie devs are so over algorithms they spent 1,500 hours listening to music on Bandcamp to compile the soundtrack
N Plus Infinity Times Two will keep the niche sonic focus of its predecessor.

author: PC Gamer
text:
GOG apologizes for emailing Nazi runes to its followers
The digital game retailer has not clearly explained how the hate symbols made it into an email, but apologized for the "unfortunate visual association."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Believe or not, this co-op and PvP zombie shooter is 'inspired by true events'
In Last Harbor you’re stranded on a boat during a zombie outbreak, but the real threat are your fellow players.
